adds.rationale: The Ukraine war is catalyzing a structural multi-year NATO rearmament cycle with members legislating record defense budgets, yet the watchlist has no geopolitical/defense exposure; ITA is the benchmark US aerospace-and-defense ETF with a 17-year track record and deep liquidity.

news_evidence.summary: Poland and Estonia push NATO allies to raise spending beyond 2% of GDP citing Russia's war; Poland legislated a 4% GDP defense budget for 2023, the highest in NATO by share of output.

World military spending grew for the eighth straight year in 2022 to an all-time high, driven by European NATO members; Germany's EUR100B Zeitenwende fund and Poland's 4% GDP target are the clearest structural signals.

removes.rationale: Apple reported its first quarterly revenue decline since 2016 in Q1 FY2023, with hardware units falling across iPhone, Mac, and iPad and the CFO guiding to a continuation; AAPL maps to no named wave (MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA cover AI, SPY covers broad markets), the weakest thematic fit at 8/8.

news_evidence.summary: Apple fiscal Q1 2023 revenue $117.2B, -5% YoY — first annual decline since 2019 — on iPhone 14 Pro China supply disruptions and a strong dollar; CFO guided to a similar declining trend for the March quarter.
Apple stock dropped on a rare earnings miss; Mac revenue fell ~30% and iPad also declined YoY, with no near-term catalyst to reverse the hardware weakness.

rationale_overall: The watchlist entered Q1 2023 with dense AI coverage (MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA) and seeded positions in every named tech next-wave (RKLB/rockets, QTUM/quantum, URA/nuclear) but zero geopolitical/defense exposure, which the Ukraine war is inflecting into a structural multi-year rearmament cycle. AAPL is the natural release valve: the only holding with no clean wave mapping, and its Feb 2023 earnings revealed the first hardware-revenue decline since 2016 with further weakness guided. Swapping AAPL for ITA fills the last uncovered wave, keeps the 8-cap, and concentrates AI where the signal is strongest — MSFT (Bing Chat, $10B OpenAI), GOOGL (Bard), NVDA (rising AI-training GPU demand).
